docs.title: PT Peek User Guide
docs.text: PT Peek is a macOS app that lets you preview and browse Pro Tools session files (.ptx) directly from Finder — without opening Pro Tools.

docs.title: What PT Peek Does
docs.text:
• Quick Look Preview — press Space on any .ptx file in Finder to see session details instantly
  • Session Browser — open .ptx files to explore tracks, clips, audio files, and plugins
  • Audio Playback — play back audio files and clips with multi-mono stereo support
  • Import to Pro Tools — import tracks, clips, and audio files directly into a running Pro Tools session
  • Output Device Selection — route playback to any audio output device on your system

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• macOS 14.0 (Sonoma) or later
  • Pro Tools 2025.6 or later (for Import to Pro Tools features)
  • iLok account (for license activation)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Install PT Peek
  2. Launch the app to register the Quick Look extension
  3. Press Space on any .ptx file in Finder to preview it
  4. Double-click or right-click → Open With → PT Peek for the full session browser

docs.title: License Activation
docs.text: PT Peek requires an iLok license for full functionality. Without a license, the Quick Look preview and session browser show limited information (session setup and overview only; tracks, clips, and audio files are redacted).

docs.title: Getting a License
docs.text:
PT Peek licenses are available through your iLok account. You need:

  1. An iLok.com account — create one at ilok.com if you don't have one
  2. A PT Peek license deposited to your account

docs.title: Activating
docs.text:
1. Open PT Peek
  2. Go to Help → Activate License…
  3. Enter your iLok Login ID
  4. Click Sign in with iLok
  5. Your browser opens to the iLok authorization page
  6. Sign in and authorize PT Peek
  7. The browser redirects back and PT Peek receives the authorization
  8. Follow the prompts to activate the license to your computer

docs.title: License Status
docs.text:
The license status appears in the session browser:

  • Licensed — all sections show full detail
  • Unlicensed — a banner appears with options to activate or try the example session
  • License expired — the helper checks every 5 seconds; if the license lapses, content is redacted until renewed

docs.title: Background Helper
docs.text:
PT Peek uses a small background helper (PT Peek Helper) for license verification:

  • Runs at login as a Login Item
  • Checks the license every 5 seconds
  • Writes a signed token that the app and Quick Look extension read
  • Uses minimal CPU and memory

You can see it in System Settings → General → Login Items under PT Peek.

Don't disable the helper

If the helper is disabled, the license token expires after 1 hour and PT Peek reverts to unlicensed mode. Re-enable it in System Settings → General → Login Items.

docs.title: Example Session
docs.text:
You can try PT Peek without a license using the built-in example session:

  • File → Download Example Session…
  • Or click "Try Example Session…" in the unlicensed banner

The example session displays with full detail regardless of license status.

docs.title: Audio Playback
docs.text: PT Peek can play audio files and clips directly from the session browser.

docs.title: Inline Playback
docs.text:
Each audio file and clip row has a small waveform display with a play button. Click the play button to start playback. Click again to pause. The waveform shows a red playhead that tracks the current position.

  • Click anywhere on the waveform to seek to that position
  • Only one item plays at a time — starting a new item stops the previous one
  • Waveform displays are cached after the first play, so subsequent views are instant

docs.title: Multi-Mono Playback
docs.text:
PT Peek automatically detects multi-mono file sets (e.g., Drums.L.wav + Drums.R.wav) and plays them together as stereo.

Multi-mono channel detection supports all standard Pro Tools suffixes:

Suffix Channel .L Left .R Right .C Center .LFE Low Frequency Effects .Ls Left Surround .Rs Right Surround .Lss, .Rss Left/Right Side Surround .Ltf, .Rtf Left/Right Top Front (Atmos) .Ltr, .Rtr Left/Right Top Rear (Atmos)

docs.title: Audio Engine
docs.text:
PT Peek uses Apple's AVAudioEngine with a Matrix Mixer audio unit for playback. This provides:

  • Direct channel routing — each mono file routes to the corresponding output channel
  • Device-aware mixing — automatically adapts to the selected output device's channel count
  • Low latency — no pre-mixing or temp file creation; files play directly from disk

docs.title: Output Device Selection
docs.text:
Use the device picker in the session browser header to select an output device. Available devices include:

  • Built-in speakers/headphones
  • USB audio interfaces
  • Bluetooth audio devices
  • Virtual audio devices (e.g., Pro Tools Audio Bridge)

The device list updates automatically when devices are connected or disconnected.

docs.title: Import to Pro Tools
docs.text: PT Peek can import tracks, audio files, and clips directly into a running Pro Tools session using the PTSL SDK.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Pro Tools 2025.6 or later must be running
  • A session must be open in Pro Tools
  • The session sample rate must match the .ptx file's sample rate
  • Audio files must be accessible on disk (online)

docs.title: From the Menu
docs.text:
1. Select one or more items (tracks, audio files, or clips)
  2. Use File → Import to Pro Tools… or press Cmd+Shift+I

docs.title: From the Context Menu
docs.text:
1. Select one or more items
  2. Right-click → Import to Pro Tools…

docs.title: From the Track Row
docs.text: Right-click any track to import it and its clips.

docs.title: Import Dialog
docs.text:
Before importing, PT Peek shows a dialog that verifies:

  1. Connection — can PT Peek connect to Pro Tools?
  2. Version — is Pro Tools 2025.6 or later?
  3. Session — is a session open? Shows the session name.
  4. Sample Rate — does the Pro Tools session match the file's sample rate?

If all checks pass, you see a list of items to import with a Cancel / Import button.

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
Importing a track creates:

  • A new track in Pro Tools with the same name, type, and format
  • All audio files used by the track's clips are imported to the clip list
  • Clips are created with the correct source offset and length
  • Clips are spotted at their original timeline positions

docs.title: Audio Files
docs.text: Importing audio files adds them to the Pro Tools clip list. No tracks are created.

docs.title: Clips
docs.text:
Importing clips:

  1. Imports the associated audio files to the clip list
  2. Creates sub-clips with the correct offset and length in the source file

No tracks are created and clips are not placed on the timeline.

docs.title: Stereo and Multi-Mono
docs.text:
PT Peek handles stereo tracks with multi-mono source files correctly:

  • Each mono file is imported separately
  • Clips reference the mono files with the correct channel routing (Left/Right)
  • The track is created with the appropriate stereo format

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
If an import fails, the dialog shows the error message from Pro Tools. Common issues:

  • "Cannot connect" — Pro Tools is not running or not responding
  • "Version not supported" — Pro Tools is older than 2025.6
  • "No session open" — open or create a session in Pro Tools
  • "Sample rate mismatch" — change the Pro Tools session sample rate to match

docs.title: Session Browser
docs.text: When you open a .ptx file with PT Peek (via Open With, double-click, or File → Open), you get the full session browser. This provides the same information as the Quick Look preview, plus interactive features.

docs.title: Session Setup
docs.text:
A grid showing session parameters, similar to the Pro Tools Session Setup window:

  • Sample Rate, Bit Depth, Audio Format
  • Session Start, Length
  • Tempo and Meter (including change indicators)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A miniature timeline view showing all tracks and their clips. Each clip is drawn as a colored rectangle matching its Pro Tools color. Tracks are stacked vertically.

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
A detailed list of every track in the session:

  • Color swatch — matches the Pro Tools track color
  • Name — track name
  • Type — Audio, Aux, Master Fader, VCA, MIDI, Instrument
  • Format — Mono, Stereo, 5.1, 7.1
  • Inserts — plugin insert slots (A through J), hover for details
  • Comments — track comments

Click a track to select it. Expand a track to see its clips.

docs.title: Plugins
docs.text: Lists all plugins used in the session, separated into Active and Inactive groups.

docs.title: Audio Files
docs.text:
All audio files referenced by the session:

  • Name — file name
  • Format — Mono, Stereo, 5.1, etc. (multi-mono files are grouped automatically)
  • Path — file path on disk
  • Waveform — inline waveform with play button (click to play)

docs.title: Clips
docs.text: All clips in the session with their source audio files. Clips from multi-mono tracks are grouped together.

docs.title: Selection
docs.text:
PT Peek supports Finder-style multi-selection:

  • Click — select one item
  • Cmd + Click — toggle an item in the selection
  • Shift + Click — extend selection to include range
  • Arrow keys — navigate up/down within a section

Selection is scoped to one section at a time. Clicking in a different section clears the previous selection.

docs.title: Audio Output Device
docs.text: The session browser includes a device picker in the header bar. Select any audio output device on your system to route playback to it.

docs.title: First Launch
docs.text: After installing PT Peek, launch it once to register the Quick Look extension with macOS.

docs.title: What Happens on First Launch
docs.text:
1. Quick Look extension registers — macOS discovers the PT Peek Quick Look extension and enables it for .ptx files
  2. License helper starts — a small background process launches to check your license status
  3. Welcome screen appears — shows key features and how to get started

You only need to launch PT Peek once

After the first launch, the Quick Look extension works in Finder even when PT Peek is not running. The background helper runs automatically at login.

docs.title: Verifying Quick Look
docs.text:
After launching PT Peek:

  1. Open Finder and navigate to a folder containing .ptx files
  2. Select a .ptx file
  3. Press Space to preview
  4. You should see the session's setup information, tracks, and more

If the preview doesn't appear, see Troubleshooting.

docs.title: Login Item
docs.text:
PT Peek installs a small background helper (PT Peek Helper) that runs at login. This helper:

  • Verifies your license status
  • Shares the license with the Quick Look extension
  • Uses minimal system resources

You can see it in System Settings → General → Login Items under PT Peek. It should be enabled for full functionality.

docs.title: Opening Sessions
docs.text:
There are several ways to open a .ptx file with PT Peek:

  • Quick Look — select the file in Finder and press Space
  • Open With — right-click the file → Open With → PT Peek
  • Drag and drop — drag a .ptx file onto the PT Peek icon in the Dock
  • File menu — use File → Open in PT Peek
  • Double-click — if PT Peek is set as the default app for .ptx files

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
Download the latest PT Peek installer from neyrinck.com/ptpeek.

The download is a macOS installer package (.pkg). It is signed and notarized by Apple, so you can install it with confidence.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
1. Double-click the .pkg file
  2. Follow the installer prompts
  3. PT Peek is installed to /Applications/PT Peek.app

Why a PKG installer instead of a DMG?

PT Peek uses a background helper for license verification. The PKG installer ensures the helper has the correct permissions to launch automatically — drag-and-drop from a DMG can cause macOS to block the helper due to quarantine restrictions.

docs.title: Uninstall
docs.text:
To remove PT Peek:

  1. Quit PT Peek if it's running
  2. Drag PT Peek.app from /Applications to the Trash
  3. The Quick Look extension and background helper are removed automatically

docs.title: Updating
docs.text: Download and run the latest installer. It will replace the previous version. Your settings and license are preserved.

docs.title: Quick Look Preview
docs.text: PT Peek adds a Quick Look extension for Pro Tools session files (.ptx). Press Space on any session file in Finder to see a preview without opening Pro Tools.

docs.title: What the Preview Shows
docs.text:
The Quick Look preview displays:

  • Session Setup — sample rate, bit depth, session length, start time
  • Overview — miniature timeline showing all tracks and clip positions
  • Tracks — list of all tracks with type, format, and plugin inserts
  • Plugins — active and inactive plugins used in the session
  • Audio Files — all audio files referenced by the session
  • Clips — all clips with their source audio files

docs.title: Sections
docs.text: Each section can be expanded or collapsed by clicking its header. PT Peek remembers which sections you had open.

docs.title: Licensed vs. Unlicensed
docs.text:
Without a license, the preview shows:

  • Session Setup and Overview — full detail
  • Tracks, Plugins, Audio Files, Clips — redacted (gray placeholder bars)
  • Section headers with counts are always visible

With a valid license, all sections show full detail.

docs.title: Example Session
docs.text:
If you don't have a license yet, you can try PT Peek with the built-in example session:

  1. In the Quick Look preview, click "Try Example Session…"
  2. Or in the app, use File → Download Example Session…
  3. The example session downloads and opens with full detail, regardless of license status

docs.title: Session Files
docs.text:
Extension Format Support .ptx Pro Tools Session (v10+) Full preview and browsing

Note

PT Peek reads .ptx files only. Older .ptf and .pts formats are not supported. Template files (.ptt) are not supported.

docs.title: Audio Formats
docs.text:
PT Peek can play audio files referenced by sessions:

Format Extensions Multi-Mono WAV .wav Yes AIFF .aif, .aiff No MP3 .mp3 No AAC .aac, .m4a No

docs.title: Multi-Mono Detection
docs.text:
PT Peek automatically groups multi-mono WAV files into stereo or surround sets based on filename suffixes:

Stereo: .L + .R

Surround formats:

  • LCR: .L + .R + .C
  • 5.1: .L + .R + .C + .LFE + .Ls + .Rs
  • 7.1: .L + .R + .C + .LFE + .Ls + .Rs + .Lss + .Rss

Atmos height channels: .Ltf, .Rtf, .Ltr, .Rtr, .Ltm, .Rtm, .Lts, .Rts

docs.title: Track Types
docs.text:
PT Peek displays all Pro Tools track types:

Type Icon Import Support Audio Track Yes Aux Input Track Yes Master Fader Track Yes VCA Track Yes MIDI Track Limited Instrument Track Yes

docs.title: Import Requirements
docs.text:
For importing to Pro Tools:

  • Pro Tools 2025.6 or later
  • Session must be open
  • Sample rates must match between the .ptx file and the Pro Tools session
  • Audio files must be accessible on disk

docs.title: Finder
docs.text: Shortcut Action Space Quick Look preview of selected .ptx file

docs.title: File Management
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd+O Open file Cmd+Shift+R Reveal selected file in Finder

docs.title: Import
docs.text: Shortcut Action Cmd+Shift+I Import selected items to Pro Tools

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Shortcut Action Up Select previous item Down Select next item Shift+Up Extend selection up Shift+Down Extend selection down Click Select item Cmd + Click Toggle item in selection Shift + Click Extend selection to item

docs.title: Playback
docs.text: Shortcut Action Space Play/pause selected audio file or clip (in session browser)

docs.title: Quick Look Preview Not Showing
docs.text:
Symptom: Pressing Space on a .ptx file shows the default icon instead of the PT Peek preview.

Solutions:

  1. Launch PT Peek once — macOS registers the Quick Look extension on first launch
  2. Check System Settings — go to Privacy & Security → Extensions → Quick Look and ensure PT Peek is enabled
  3. Reset Quick Look — open Terminal and run: 
    qlmanage -r
  4. Re-register the app — open Terminal and run: 
    /System/Library/Frameworks/CoreServices.framework/Versions/Current/Frameworks/LaunchServices.framework/Versions/Current/Support/lsregister -f -R -trusted /Applications/PT\ Peek.app

docs.title: License Not Detected
docs.text:
Symptom: The app shows "License not found" even though you have a valid license.

Solutions:

  1. Check the helper — open Activity Monitor and search for "PT Peek Helper". It should be running. If not:
    • Go to System Settings → General → Login Items
    • Ensure PT Peek is enabled
    • Quit and relaunch PT Peek
  2. Reset background task management — if the helper won't start: 
    sudo sfltool resetbtm
    Then quit and relaunch PT Peek.
  3. Check Keychain — run from Terminal: 
    "/Applications/PT Peek.app/Contents/MacOS/PT Peek" --check-keychain
    This shows whether the secret key and token are present.
  4. Clear and restart — if all else fails: 
    "/Applications/PT Peek.app/Contents/MacOS/PT Peek" --clear-keychain
"/Applications/PT Peek.app/Contents/MacOS/PT Peek" --unregister-helper
    Then relaunch PT Peek.

docs.title: Import to Pro Tools Fails
docs.text:
"Cannot connect to Pro Tools"

  • Make sure Pro Tools is running
  • Pro Tools must be version 2025.6 or later

"No session open"

  • Open or create a session in Pro Tools before importing

"Sample rate mismatch"

  • The Pro Tools session sample rate must match the .ptx file
  • Change the Pro Tools session sample rate, or open a session that matches

"Version not supported"

  • Import requires Pro Tools 2025.6 or later
  • Update Pro Tools to the latest version

docs.title: Audio Playback Issues
docs.text:
No sound

  • Check the output device picker in the session browser header
  • Make sure the selected device is not muted
  • Verify the audio files are accessible on disk (not offline)

Wrong channels / panning

  • PT Peek routes mono files directly to output channels
  • For multi-mono, ensure files have the correct channel suffixes (.L, .R, etc.)

docs.title: App Crashes or Freezes
docs.text:
If PT Peek becomes unresponsive:

  1. Force quit: Cmd+Option+Esc → select PT Peek → Force Quit
  2. Check Console.app for crash logs under PT Peek
  3. Report the issue with the crash log

docs.title: Debug Commands
docs.text:
These can be run from Terminal for advanced troubleshooting:

APP="/Applications/PT Peek.app/Contents/MacOS/PT Peek"

# Check license token status
"$APP" --check-keychain

# Delete token file (keeps secret key)
"$APP" --delete-token

# Delete both secret key and token
"$APP" --clear-keychain

# Unregister the background helper
"$APP" --unregister-helper
